required_capabilities: warn or fail closed when checksum verification is unavailable
render or expose index.html and index.txt when supported
list sources, evals, routes, and pages from summary files
handle missing optional files without treating the snapshot as corrupt

required_validation_steps: Read README_FIRST.txt before interpreting the snapshot.
Parse SNAPSHOT_MANIFEST.json and confirm snapshot_format_version is supported.
Confirm the manifest flags do not claim a production signed release, real signing keys, real binaries, live backend, live probes, or external observations.
Parse BUILD_MANIFEST.json for build provenance and static/export limitations.
Verify CHECKSUMS.SHA256 entries for all files when the consumer can compute SHA-256.
Parse SOURCE_SUMMARY.json, EVAL_SUMMARY.json, ROUTE_SUMMARY.json, and PAGE_REGISTRY.json only after manifest and checksum checks.
Read SIGNATURES.README.txt as placeholder documentation and do not treat v0 as a real signed release.
Report unsupported features as unavailable rather than silently ignoring them.
optional_validation_steps: Render index.html when the client supports HTML.
Render index.txt when the client supports plain text.
Compare public data counts with manifest source_counts where available.
Record local validation output outside the snapshot tree.

checksum_policy.authenticity_limit: Checksums delivered with the same untrusted snapshot are not a complete authenticity proof.

must_not_do: treat missing optional docs as corruption
fetch missing files from the network

prohibited_assumptions: v0 signatures are real production signatures
checksums alone prove authenticity when delivered over an untrusted channel
missing optional files can be fetched automatically
a snapshot is a production API
notes: Signed Snapshot Consumer Contract v0 defines future consumption behavior only.
No production, native, or relay consumer is implemented by this contract.
Future old-browser, text, file-tree, relay, native, and audit consumers should share the same read order, checksum posture, and v0 signature-placeholder limitations.
Consumers must not perform network fetches, live probes, scraping, or arbitrary local filesystem ingestion to fill missing snapshot data.
